Sports.title: Lindsey Vonn admits she nearly went 'insane' in Italian hospital after Winter Olympics crash as stunning skier gives harrowing details to Vanity Fair
Sports.content:
Lindsey Vonn admits she nearly went 'insane' in Italian hospital after Winter Olympics crash as stunning skier gives harrowing details to Vanity Fair

Lindsey Vonn has admitted she nearly went 'insane' while recovering in an Italian hospital following the horrifying Winter Olympics crash that nearly cost her a leg.

In a new interview with Vanity Fair, the skier has described in harrowing detail how much pain she was in and revealed it was a Team USA doctor who performed the crucial surgery that spared her left leg.

The USA icon, 41, made a miraculous comeback at Milan-Cortina last month only to suffer a horrific broken leg 13 seconds into her run in the women's downhill final on February 8. She had already torn her ACL before the Games.

After the first of five surgeries, Vonn suffered from compartment syndrome which left doctors facing a race against time to avoid having to amputate the limb. It was Tom Hackett, the head physician for Team USA Ski and Snowboard, who performed the vital fasciotomy.

Following multiple surgeries in both Italy and the United States, the skiing legend was allowed to return home to Utah on March 1, almost a month after the crash happened.

Speaking to Vanity Fair, Vonn admitted the pain is 'seared into my brain' and it took everything she had to not go insane while in hospital in the Italian city of Treviso.

Lindsey Vonn has given harrowing details of her shocking Winter Olympics crash to Vanity Fair

Vonn needed five surgeries in total after she came dangerously close to losing her leg

Detailing an initial CT scan at the Olympic clinic before they even went to the hospital, Vonn said: 'Halfway through, I started sweating. I was just in such extreme pain.

'I screamed at the top of my lungs: Get me out. It just wouldn't dissipate. It wouldn't let up. It's seared into my brain.'

After flying to Treviso via helicopter, they were unable to land at the hospital at first because paparazzi had gathered on the helipad. When they eventually touched down, a team of 20 doctors and nurses were ready to treat Vonn.

The first surgery went 'great', according to Hackett, but Vonn later woke up in the middle of the night screaming louder than she had initially after the crash.

Her leg was swelling, thanks to the compartment syndrome, and the pain was not being reduced by large amounts of fentanyl, morphine and oxycodone.

Detailing the decision to operate again, Hackett added: 'There was a very significant chance that she was going to lose all function of her leg, if not the leg itself. Best-case scenario in those situations is, you might keep your leg, but it’s going to be useless.'

Hackett successfully performed the fasciotomy but her entire experience in that hospital in Treviso, of being in such agonizing pain and on vast amounts of medication, has left Vonn with psychological scars.

She recalls how nurses were checking on her every three hours, there were other patients on the ward with her and the lights stayed on until 11pm. Vonn says: 'It took everything I had for it to not drive me insane.'

Join the debate Should elite athletes risk their long-term health for a chance at Olympic glory?

The skiing legend suffered a broken left leg after she crashed in the women's downhill

The 41-year-old later fired back at the 'haters' who said she was 'selfish' to ski at the Games

When she finally returned home to Park City in Utah, Vonn was sent emotional letters from the likes of David Beckham, Jannik Sinner, Tom Brady and Prince William.

A segment of Prince William's letter shared in the interview reads: 'The way you wrote about stepping into the starting gate with courage and no regrets says so much about your resilience.'

Vonn has since proven that she has bounced back faster than fans expected as she showed off her incredible progress. The former gold medalist was back in the gym last week, sharing a video of her workout to social media on Saturday.

Her current rehabilitation schedule includes a two-hour physio session every day, followed by two hours in a hyperbaric chamber and then a gym workout back at home.

When asked if she is even thinking about returning to the slopes once again, she added: 'I don’t like to close the door on anything, because you just never know what’s going to happen.



'It’s hard to tell with this injury. It’s so f***ed up. I really feel like that was a horrible last run to end my career on. I only made it 13 seconds. But they were a really good 13 seconds.'

Sports.title: Ryan Moore gives verdict on star racehorse Constitution Hill’s ‘Group race’ prospects
Sports.content:
Ryan Moore gives verdict on star racehorse Constitution Hill’s ‘Group race’ prospects

The top jockey rode the former Champion Hurdle winner to his second victory on the Flat in front of a large crowd of fans at Kempton on Wednesday

View 3 Images Ryan Moore acknowledges the crowd after winning on Constitution Hill (Image: PA)

Ryan Moore has offered a view on whether star racehorse Constitution Hill can cut it at Group race level on the Flat. The top jockey rode the former Champion Hurdle winner for the first time in a novice stakes over 1m4f at Kempton Park on Monday.

‌



A crowd of 1,477, more than three times the average Wednesday night attendance at the Sunbury track, turned up to see him and their idol did not let them down as he was pushed out to record a two length victory at odds of 1-7. The once invincible jumper, who had fallen three times in four races over jumps, won at Southwell 33 days earlier.

‌



Afterwards Moore said: “He moved well and I think there's a bit more improvement to come so we'd be happy with that. We don't know what level he can compete at. We'll know more next time. I think he is very comfortable at 12f.”

‌



Constitution Hurdle won ten in a row in the jumps sphere before problems surfaced last season. At Cheltenham in January he made a mistake at the last hurdle but stayed on his feet to win.

Then in the Champion Hurdle he fell at the fourth, fell again at the second last at Aintree and went at the second during the Fighting Fifth in November.

View 3 Images Constitution Hill (centre): winning for a second time on the Flat (Image: PA)

‌



He made his Flat debut in February when he recorded a stunning win at Southwell, scoring by seven lengths, a performance which persuaded trainer Nicky Henderson not to risk him suffering another fall at Cheltenham but to continue on the Flat.

The nine-year-old is likely to step up significantly in class on his next start to tackle the Group 3 John Porter Stakes at Newbury on April 18.

View 3 Images Nicky Henderson with the victorious Constitution Hill at Kempton (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Moore has shared more of his thoughts on Constitution Hill’s potential in his World Pool blog.

He said: “Constitution Hill is a gentleman. He’s an old pro and a very sweet horse – he’s a lovely ride.

“He had a simple task at Kempton and he did it well. He moved beautifully and did everything right; he travelled nicely, quickened up well and you’d be very happy with the performance.

Article continues below

“It will be step by step and his next run will tell us a lot more, but he’s certainly got enough pace to be competitive in Group races at a mile-and-a-half.”

Sports.title: Tommy Fleetwood teases new partnership after end of long-standing relationship
Sports.content:
Tommy Fleetwood teases new partnership after end of long-standing relationship

Golf star Tommy Fleetwood saw a long-standing relationship come to an end in late 2025 but a new announcement appears imminent

View Image Tommy Fleetwood saw one partnership end in late 2025 (Image: TGL Golf via Getty Images)

British golfer Tommy Fleetwood has hinted he could soon announce a new endorsement deal just a few weeks after his agreement with Nike ended. His brand deal expired at the end of 2025 and he confirmed in February that it wasn't being renewed.

‌



"Our contract was ending and it just turned out like we sort of didn’t end up in a place where I’m wearing Nike stuff this year," Fleetwood said during an appearance at the Pebble Beach Pro-Am. "Who knows what’s going to happen in the future. But this is cool.

‌



"I don’t know, I feel like I’m a big golf fan. I really like golf stuff, so it’s quite nice to be wearing Pebble Beach clothing. I mean, my kids would absolutely love this stuff so I feel pretty lucky to have it on."

‌



At the time, the Merseyside pro suggested he was in no rush to agree a new partnership. That hasn't stopped speculation, though, and now the wait appears to be nearly over.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

Tommy Fleetwood's son steals the show as Tiger Woods denied millions READ MORE :

Fleetwood teased a new deal with a short video on Instagram. The clip, in which he is seen looking at golf clothing, is captioned: 'Something new coming…stay tuned!'.

‌



The news brought a mixed reception from fans. "Oh no, I like free agent Tommy" said one, while another joked that he should play in "Full Everton kit" in honour of the football team he supports.

There has also been some speculation about the gear he will wear going forward. Some expect him to sign with Lululemon or Sun Day Red after being snapped in those brands' gear while others even wondered if he might be launching his own golf clothing line.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



In February, LIV Golf player Ben An joked that Fleetwood might want to be decked out in the gear of his Korean Golf Club team. An, 34, joined LIV in January.

"Looks like Tommy Fleetwood is a free agent on clothing. Let me know if you want some Korean Golf Club clothes," the Seoul-born star wrote on social media. "Happy to send you some. Relax, people, it's a joke... maybe."

Southport-born Fleetwood dropped from third to fourth in the world rankings after Cam Young won The Players Championship to overtake him. However, he already has three top-10 finishes in PGA Tour events this year and tied for 8th at TPC Sawgrass.

Article continues below

The 35-year-old secured a huge $10m (£7.5m) payday for his win at the Tour Championship last year. Remarkably, it was also his first ever PGA Tour win despite millions in career earnings up to that point.

"When you've lost so many times, a three-shot lead down the last doesn't feel like that many," he joked after the victory. "[The fans] are amazing, it makes me a bit emotional. I'm so lucky with the support I get - it's so special and I hope everyone knows how grateful I am for it."

Sports.title: Furious Gerwyn Price releases statement after being beaten by Luke Littler again
Sports.content:
Furious Gerwyn Price releases statement after being beaten by Luke Littler again

Gerwyn Price was again undone by Luke Littler in the Premier League but was left frustrated by the conditions at Berlin's Uber Arena, which has copped criticism in the past

View 2 Images Gerwyn Price let rip at the conditions in Berlin (Image: James Fearn/Getty Images)

Gerwyn Price took aim at the conditions in Berlin after he was beaten by Luke Littler on night eight of the Premier League.

‌



The Welshman has struggled to get over the line against the teenager as of late. Last week he let a 5-0 lead slip before losing to The Nuke in the final and this week, in the German capital, he was undone 6-4 as Littler claimed another nightly victory.

‌



After the loss, Price didn't hold back about the conditions inside the Uber Arena, which has attracted criticism in the past. Players have complained about the breeze in previous years, which impacts on the flight of their darts.

‌



The Iceman didn't have much faith in things changing though. He said on Instagram: “So bloody frustrating. Luke's playing really well atm. But to have to play in such shocking conditions it's so bloody hard.

"But nothing's ever done… Maybe it be easier to play on the beach front outdoors every week so we know what to expect… Thanks all for the amazing support once again you really are amazing. Onto next week it is.”

‌



Luke Littler responds to German darts fans booing him as anger made clear READ MORE :

Luke Littler hosts darts tournament for England stars at St George's Park READ MORE :

Littler was able to navigate any such conditions and ended the night as the winner, which has allowed him to take over at the top of the table following Jonny Clayton's early exit. Price and Michael Van Gerwen make up the top four.

The World champion then claimed that, after Clayton's fast start, that he was now relishing being the man to catch. He said: "It's been very interesting this Premier League. Obviously Jonny [Clayton] ran away with it a little bit, but I thought there was going to be a much bigger gap while I was sat seventh. I was also sat at sixth at one point but now they can all try and catch me!"

View 2 Images Luke Littler has moved to the top of the Premier League table

Article continues below

Littler also claimed his performance in Germany was perhaps his greatest across the Premier League circuit, with a change of darts proving key.

"It was a very good night overall," Littler explained. "I played some outstanding darts - I think that's the best I've ever played for a [Premier League] night and got the job done.

"With the new darts, when I was practising, I was hitting bull near enough every three darts at least once. Before the 170s I hit a 126 hit on the bull. I just feel really confident with these new darts but we've just got to back it up next week. Stats-wise and obviously how I played and how I feel, this is the best I've felt in a while."

Sports.title: Sir Chris Hoy speaks out about cancer diagnosis as he delivers urgent message
Sports.content:
Sir Chris Hoy speaks out about cancer diagnosis as he delivers urgent message

British cycling hero Sir Chris Hoy has issued an urgent plea after being diagnosed with terminal cancer

View 2 Images Sir Chris Hoy was told he had between two to four years after being diagnosed with terminal prostate cancer (Image: PA)

Sir Chris Hoy has issued an urgent message for men to undergo prostate cancer screening. The British cycling legend received the life-changing news in September 2023 that he had prostate cancer.

‌



Doctors found a tumour after the Team GB hero felt discomfort in his shoulder and ribs. Scans then showed primary cancer in his prostate had metastasised to his bones.

‌



The 50-year-old announced in October 2024 that his cancer had already progressed to stage four, meaning his condition was terminal and he had an estimated two to four years to live. Since then, Hoy has found a new purpose outside of cycling.

‌



He has committed himself to raising awareness of prostate cancer and inspiring people to get more screenings. The six-time Olympic gold medallist recently launched the Scottish Prostate Cancer Initiative in Glasgow - a programme designed to screen 25,000 Scottish men.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

The initiative aims to drastically improve early detection and diagnosis rates, which will in turn save lives. Sir Chris is urging men across Scotland, especially those over the age of 40, to get a simple test.

‌



He said: "I think the most important thing is to explain that it’s just a simple blood test. With a PSA [prostate specific antigen] test you’re in and out of the doctor’s in five minutes. It’s easy, it’s simple, it’s painless and it could save your life."

Speaking about how the prognosis impacted him, Hoy said: "A stage four diagnosis changes your life drastically but I guess it’s finding a new purpose, and today’s event is all about trying to educate men about the importance of catching prostate cancer early.

View 2 Images The Olympic cycling legend has launched the Scottish Prostate Cancer Initiative (Image: PA)

Article continues below

"Early detection saves lives – that is the message to take away from today. I always assumed there would be some kind of symptoms or early-warning signs. My grandpa had prostate cancer, my dad had it, so I was aware of it.

"But even then, we’re always told it’s 50 years and above, that it’s an older man’s disease and it’s slow-moving, and you’ll get warning signs. That’s not always the case. So, I think that that is the key thing."

Hoy's efforts to raise awareness in recent years have yielded remarkable results, with a reported 700 per cent increase in inquiries from men to their GPs for test bookings since he announced he has terminal cancer.

Sports.title: Donald Trump leaks if Tiger Woods will be playing the Masters on live TV
Sports.content:
Donald Trump leaks if Tiger Woods will be playing the Masters on live TV

As the start of the Masters begins to creep closer, US president Donald Trump has appeared to reveal if Tiger Woods will be competing at Augusta next month

View 3 Images Donald Trump appeared to drop a major hint about whether Tiger Woods will be at the Masters (Image: YouTube/Sky News)

US president Donald Trump has claimed Tiger Woods will not be competing at the Masters. Woods, 50, has been intent on overcoming his injury problems to compete at Augusta next month.

‌



With the major beginning on Thursday, April 9, Woods does not have long to overcome his physical barriers. The 15-time major winner has made it clear he is doing everything he can to compete, having not featured in a major competition since the Open in 2024.

‌



Woods did compete in the SoFi Cup, his first time in competition in over a year, but his Jupiter Links side were beaten convincingly by the Los Angeles Golf Club. After his side's loss, he addressed his chances of participating in Georgia.

‌



Your unofficial preview to The Masters 2026 is ON SALE NOW READ MORE :

Roy Keane reacts exactly as expected after Rory McIlroy delivers thoughtful gift READ MORE :

However, Trump has insisted Woods will not be in action at the Masters. The White House chief did not reveal his sources, but Woods is in a relationship with Vanessa Trump, the president's former daughter-in-law.

Trump told Fox News: "I love Tiger, but he won't be there. He'll be there, but he won't be playing in it.”

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Woods is yet to confirm whether he will be competing at the Masters, though his profile on the tournament's official website states he will be competing. He has made it clear he will be at Augusta in some capacity and will attend the Champion's Dinner.

The golf great has undergone seven back surgeries and also ruptured his left Achilles tendon in March 2025. After his side's SoFi Cup loss, Woods did open up about how hard he is working to be fit to compete.

View 3 Images Woods was in action at the SoFi Cup, albeit his side were beaten convincingly (Image: 2026 Jamie Squire/TGL)

‌



He said: "I've been trying. It's just this body doesn't recover like when I was 24, 25. It doesn't mean I'm not trying - I've been trying for a while.

"I've had a couple of back injuries the past year that I've had to fight through and it's taken some time. But I keep trying.

View 3 Images Trump and Woods know each other well (Image: 2025 Getty Images)

Article continues below

"I want to play, I've loved the tournament, I've loved being there since I was 19 years old. So it's meant a lot to me and my family over the years and I'm going to be there either way.”

When asked if it will be a late decision, perhaps one in the days counting up to the start of the major, Woods replied: “I don't know, we'll see how it goes. I'll be practicing playing at home this week and keep trying to make progress.”

Sports.title: Rory McIlroy refused Bryson DeChambeau request at Masters as he speaks out on disagreement
Sports.content:
Rory McIlroy refused Bryson DeChambeau request at Masters as he speaks out on disagreement

Tensions between Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau reached new heights at the Masters during a decisive moment

View 2 Images Bryson DeChambeau and Rory McIlroy had a disagreement at the 2025 Masters (Image: Getty Images)

Rory McIlroy has revealed how he refused to give in to pressure from Bryson DeChambeau in the lead up to his Masters victory last year. In the final round of the 2025 Masters, McIlroy’s desperate attempt to finally win at Augusta National saw him implement a new tactic.

‌



The Northern Irishman, both in an effort to maintain focus and to frustrate his opponent, refused to make eye contact with DeChambeau and treated his rival as if he wasn’t there, even wearing AirPods until the moment he stepped onto the first tee. The American had tried to bait him into conversation at various points in order to break his rhythm, leading to a tense atmosphere by the ninth hole.

‌



Both players hit brilliant approaches at the steep uphill par-4, leaving themselves around 12 to 15 feet away for birdie. But there was a disagreement over who was furthest away and who would therefore putt first.

‌



Instead of the usual quick word or a hand gesture to decide the order, McIlroy continued the silent treatment until DeChambeau suggested leaving it to chance by throwing a tee to determine who would go first. McIlroy refused, leaving the LIV Golf star to measure the respective distances with his steps before finally giving in.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

The eventual champ drained the putt to a deafening roar from the crowd, while a visibly agitated DeChambeau stepped up and pushed his putt wide right. That shifted the momentum in McIlroy’s favour as he mounted a four-shot lead.

‌



Golf news: Tiger Woods makes Masters admission as Scottie Scheffler pulls out of event READ MORE :

Is Tiger Woods playing at The Masters after huge hint as he makes golf comeback READ MORE :

Recalling the moment of friction in Amazon Prime’s new documentary, Rory McIlroy: The Masters Wait, he said: "I thought it was very clearly my putt.

"I thought his ball was slightly closer than mine. We sort of looked at each other and I’m like, ‘I think it’s me to go,’ and he’s like, ‘Well, I think it’s me to go.’

"It’s a very gamesmanship-y thing. Really, both of us wanted to putt first. Because if you can hole that putt before your opponent it puts pressure on them.

‌



View 2 Images McIlroy came out on top in a decisive moment at Augusta (Image: Getty)

"He goes, ‘So why don’t we just throw a tee up for it to see who goes up first?’ And I’m like, ‘No, this is the final round of the Masters, this isn’t some game on a Tuesday afternoon somewhere.’

"I wasn’t going to wilt in that situation, I was just going to stand firm. So I said, ‘Why don’t we get someone to come and measure the distance,' and he said, ‘No, no it’s fine. You can go anyway. I don’t care.’

‌



"I was proud of myself for holding my ground."

That moment marked a turning point for DeChambeau as he looked increasingly out of sorts. Later, airing his frustration, he said: "Rory wouldn't talk to me. Not a word. I get being focused but that’s just not me. It felt cold out there."

McIlroy went on to claim his first ever Green Jacket and complete the career Grand Slam following a play-off win over Justin Rose. The five-time major champion was unapologetic in the aftermath, saying: "I wasn’t there to be his mate. I was there to win the Masters. If he wanted a conversation, he should’ve booked a lunch."

Article continues below
